free_text: fast neutron induced fission of 235U and 237Np
100 microg/cm**2 thick layers of uranium dioxide
or neptunium dioxide evaporated onto 30 microg/cm**2
thick carbon backings.  Mass 150 microg of each.
Pu-239  less than 0.015  Pu-240  less than 0.002
U      less than 0.1    Th      less than 0.02
Time Of Flight with four different
Pulse heights were converted to kinetic energies
using a corrected version of the calibration
method of Schmidt et al.,Proc. Symp. on Physics
and Chemistry of Fission, IAEA, Salzburg,
All distributions are given with respect to primary
fragment masses A* before neutron evaporation.
Mass intervals in all tables are 0.5 amu for which
the analysis was carried out. The experimental mass
resolution was limited to 2.1 amu. Figures show
distributions in mass intervals of 1 amu.
The following were taken into account :
Fission fragment energy loss in the sample,
Free text was corrected to be "human-readable".
SD: SOLST -> SIBAR in DETECTOR code.
Changes in REACTION code in Subents 11,21.
STATUS "COREL" deleted, info moved to REL-REF.
